events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Scenario: ABS-to-PVC glued with wrong cement. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Field condition: ABS-to-PVC glued with wrong cement. Work rough-complete. Need assess compliance per IPC and mfr listings.

We need know plumbing code: joining ABS and PVC. IPC? ABS and PVC are different thermoplastics. Solvent cement for PVC is not for ABS; ABS uses ABS solvent cement or all-purpose? Actually transition fittings: There are approved transition couplings or special fittings listed for joining ABS to PVC. Using wrong cement (PVC cement on ABS?) is non-compliant. IPC 605.16? Let's recall IPC sections: Plastic pipe and fittings joining. ABS and PVC cannot be solvent welded together unless using approved transition cement/fittings. IPC 701.3? For drainage, ABS to PVC joints require approved adapter fittings or transition couplings, not direct solvent cementing with wrong cement. Manufacturer listings require proper cement per pipe material. So decision fail. Risk high? A glued transition with wrong cement can leak sewer gas/fluids, structural failure. Could be high. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 80? Value not asked 0. Sound etc default 0/empty. Findings: ABS-to-PVC joint made with incompatible solvent cement; not listed for transition; violates IPC and manufacturer instructions. Actions: remove/replace joint with approved transition fitting or correct listed cement per manufacturer; re-inspect. Rationale brief. References: IPC (e.g., IPC 605.16/705.6?), mfr listings. Need exact anchors? We can include generic. The instruction says apply rather than quote mechanically. We can reference "IPC - plastic pipe joints shall be made with materials listed for the specific pipe/fitting materials; ABS-to-PVC transitions require approved adapter/transition fittings or listed transition cement per manufacturer". Good.

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.
